title: Decadent Egyptian Dates Pudding
description: A roasted, fluffy, sugar‑free Egyptian date pudding made with heavy cream, almond flour, and a mortar‑pestle date paste, perfect for a romantic date‑night dessert.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and spread 200g pitted dates on a baking sheet to roast for 5 minutes until lightly caramelized.
Remove the dates and let them cool slightly before pounding them with a mortar and pestle until they form a smooth paste.
In a medium saucepan, heat 150ml heavy cream, 30g unsalted butter, and 150ml water over medium heat until the butter melts and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
Stir in the almond flour gradually, whisking continuously to avoid lumps and allowing the mixture to thicken into a creamy base.
Add the pounded dates, 1 tsp cardamom powder, 1 tsp rose water, a pinch of sea salt, and the cinnamon stick, stirring until fully integrated.
Reduce the heat to low and let the pudding simmer gently for 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a thick, glossy consistency.
Remove the cinnamon stick and pour the pudding into a hot‑pot vessel, such as a small Dutch oven or heat‑proof bowl.
Cover the vessel with a lid or foil and allow the pudding to set for 10 minutes at room temperature, then chill in the refrigerator for an additional 30 minutes.
Serve each portion garnished with a few whole dates, a dusting of crushed almonds, and a drizzle of rose water if desired.
Enjoy this decadent, fluffy, sugar‑free Egyptian date pudding as a romantic date‑night dessert.
